headline: No-limit option for per-user and per-stash upload caps
summary: Upload-limit overrides can now lift a cap entirely instead of only retuning it, on both individual users and customer stash pages.

items.title: Mark a per-user upload cap as No limit
items.body: Each per-user override — storage quota, max file size, max bundle size, max file count, and allowed extensions — now has a No-limit toggle in the user-limits dialog. A blank field still falls back to the global default; No limit removes the cap for that user, letting an admin grant an individual user a higher ceiling than the instance-wide setting. A No-limit storage quota also overrides a configured global per-user quota, and marking allowed extensions No limit accepts any file type for that user.

items.title: No-limit upload caps on customer stash pages
items.body: The stash editor gains the same No-limit toggle on max file size, max bundle size, max files, and allowed extensions. It now also exposes a Max Bundle Size field, which previously had no control.

items.title: Reorganized the stash editor
items.body: The customer-stash editor is wider and grouped into Page, Access, Upload limits, and Sync sections for a clearer layout.

items.title: Updated vendored framework
items.body: Refreshed the vendored blamejs dependency to its latest release.
